channel.title: LIBRCHAIN 区块链新闻雷达
channel.description: 每小时自动更新的区块链新闻、博客与 X 热点聚合

item.title: HYPE chases new highs as ETF inflows, institutional adoption accelerate
item.description: HYPE reached new highs above $65 following record ETF inflows, futures participation and trading volumes at Hyperliquid exchange. Is $100 next?

item.title: Crypto advocacy group challenges Senator Warren's claims on OCC charters
item.description: After Massachusetts Senator Elizabeth Warren's claimed that the Comptroller may have violated US banking laws by approving crypto companies’ charters, The Digital Chamber urged the regulator to defend the applications.

item.title: Bitcoin mining stocks jump as AI infrastructure boom boosts sector outlook
item.description: Wall Street’s semiconductor-driven surge is fueling fresh momentum for crypto miners betting their power-heavy infrastructure can support the AI boom.

item.title: Some Non-Enhanced Athletes Beat Their Juiced Rivals at the 'Steroid Olympics'
item.description: The inaugural games produced a record-breaking swim, but several non-enhanced athletes still beat the enhanced competitors.

item.title: Trump praises prediction markets, defends CFTC as court cases compound
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said it was "critically important" that the CFTC keep "exclusive authority" over prediction markets, echoing CFTC Chair Michael Selig.

item.title: Someone Just Destroyed $8.2 Million in Bitcoin—Why?
item.description: Five unknown addresses removed 107 Bitcoin worth $8.2 million from circulation today, sparking intrigue on social media.

item.title: This Half-Gigabyte AI Model Runs Local Agents on Your Phone
item.description: OpenBMB's 1B-parameter model brings MCP support and agentic tool use to on-device AI—but it has trouble with logic traps.

item.title: Ethereum Firm Sharplink, Solana Treasury Forward Industries Joining Russell 2000, 3000 Indexes
item.description: Crypto treasury firms Sharplink and Forward Industries will both be included in the Russell 2000 and 3000 indexes at the end of June.

item.title: Grand Theft Data: Threat Actors Weaponizing GTA 6 Hype, NordVPN Warns
item.description: Cybercriminals are weaponizing the hype surrounding Grand Theft Auto 6, flooding the internet with GTA phishing traps and malware.

item.title: Spain Blocks Prediction Markets Kalshi, Polymarket for Potential Gambling Law Violations
item.description: Spain is the latest country to clamp down on the rising use of prediction market platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi.

item.title: Joe Lubin-backed Ethereum treasury firm SharpLink to join the Russel indexes
item.description: The inclusion could attract fresh passive inflows from index-tracking funds, while the firm's stock tanked 95% from its peak over the past year.

item.title: AI Chatbots Show Bias Toward Catholicism, Researchers Say
item.description: Researchers find AI models steer users toward Catholicism and away from other religions, like Jehovah’s Witnesses.

item.title: UK authorities sanction HTX crypto exchange over support for Russia
item.description: Formerly Huobi Global, the exchange is the latest entity to be named as part of a crackdown on companies “exploited by Russia to circumvent UK sanctions.”

item.title: TeraWulf Stock Pops as Bitcoin Miner Acquires Kentucky Site to Meet AI Power Demands
item.description: Bitcoin miner TeraWulf’s latest deal adds more than a gigawatt of potential data center capacity in a region eager for economic investment.

item.title: Inaudible Audio Attacks Can Hijack AI Voice Models, Study Finds
item.description: Researchers were able to embed hidden signals in audio clips that silently commandeer AI model behavior.

item.title: UK Issues Sanctions Against Justin Sun's HTX, Other Crypto Firms Over Alleged Russia Ties
item.description: British financial institutions are now barred from doing business with the exchanges—and may face penalties for interacting with crypto transactions that pass through them.

item.title: TeraWulf acquires Kentucky AI data center site with planned 1 GW capacity
item.description: WULF shares surged on Tuesday after the Bitcoin miner announced its latest move to expand into AI and HPC through a multi-phase buildout through 2030.

item.title: UK sanctions Huobi and ruble stablecoin issuer in crackdown on Russia crypto networks
item.description: Britain applied banking-style sanctions to crypto exchanges for the first time, requiring U.K. financial firms to freeze funds and trace transactions.

item.title: Spanish authorities block Polymarket and Kalshi over gambling laws
item.description: Spain joined a growing list of national authorities restricting or banning prediction market platforms amid concerns over unlicensed gambling activities.

item.title: Bitcoin 'liquidation hunts' control BTC at $77K amid new Iran peace deal doubts
item.description: Bitcoin failed to copy US stocks' upside after a spike to $78,000 became one of a series of liquidity grabs.

item.title: NEAR token price has 'potential to grow 20x,' says Arthur Hayes
item.description: NEAR is mirroring a 2023–2024 fractal that preceded a 250% rally, hinting at similar upside in 2026 as privacy coin demand grows.

item.title: Bermuda, the small island nation with huge crypto ambitions
item.description: The government recently announced it would become the world’s first economy to go fully onchain, a move officials believe will bring its people new opportunities.

item.title: Coinbase’s Base launches AI tool for ChatGPT to manage crypto wallets and DeFi apps
item.description: The product, called Base MCP, connects a user’s Base Account to AI clients such as ChatGPT, Claude and Cursor using the Model Context Protocol (MCP), an emerging standard that allows AI systems to securely interface with external tools and…

item.title: Tom Lee's BitMine Makes Biggest Ethereum Buy Yet in 2026
item.description: BitMine Immersion Technologies made its largest Ethereum acquisition yet this year—just weeks after saying it might slow down its ETH buys.

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

item.title: XRPL could close its biggest DeFi gap if new AMM amendment passes
item.description: A draft proposal filed Tuesday would extend the XRP Ledger's native automated market maker with three swappable curve types, giving liquidity providers more efficient ways to deploy capital and closing one of XRPL DeFi's longest-standing g…

item.title: Indonesia Blocks Polymarket After Bets on President’s Early Departure
item.description: The ban came on the heels of markets linked to the early departure of Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto.

item.title: StepFun's Voice AI Topped Every Benchmark. It Also Hears Your Sighs
item.description: The Shanghai lab that builds LLMs that punch above their weight just turned that same energy on voice—and the results are hard to ignore.

item.title: AI guardrail removals raise questions over limits of open-source model regulation
item.description: Financial Times testing found safety controls on open AI models from Meta and Google could be stripped in minutes, raising governance concerns.

item.title: The Clarity Act won’t lead to adoption without crypto tax reform
item.description: The legislation is being framed as a turning point for U.S. crypto policy, but there’s a major piece of the puzzle being overlooked, argues Singh.

item.title: Strategy buys back $1.5B of debt at discount, cuts outstanding notes to $6.7B
item.description: Strategy reduced aggregate convertible notes outstanding to $6.7 billion after repurchasing 2029 notes for $1.38 billion in cash.

item.title: Grvt adds 3 tokenized yield funds tied to institutional-grade RWAs
item.description: Grvt will work with Plume to launch tokenized fixed-income and structured credit products tied to institutional-grade real world assets and onchain yield strategies.

item.title: China Imposes Travel Limits on AI Workers at Private Firms: Report
item.description: Beijing is reportedly making some private-sector AI workers seek travel approval, widening its control over tech talent.

item.title: ALIENS ARE BUYING BITCOIN The city of Roswell, New Mexico was the site of a famous UFO crash in 1947. The US Army Air Force announced possession of a “flying disc”, believed by many to be an alien spacecraft. Now, that same city holds $13.3K of BTC - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiW0FVX3lxTE5HejNCRjBKNHdqdkNySGlYbG15aGJ0cHF3NEhsaFd0OHlkeDRvUnl4UllYeEJLclM1TDBaTTRldEd5MTU4RGRNWE40THBCSTdodlNkNlowa21STW8?oc=5" target="_blank">ALIENS ARE BUYING BITCOIN The city of Roswe…

item.title: RWA market hits $51B as tokenized private credits surges: Bernstein
item.description: Bernstein Research data shows the RWA market at $51 billion, with tokenized private credit leading and Figure leading RWA platforms with $18 billion in tokenized assets.

item.title: TeraWulf jumps 13% on AI data center expansion in Kentucky
item.description: The bitcoin miner turned HPC infrastructure developer unveiled plans for a new 1 gigawatt facility in Kentucky aimed at servicing AI workloads.

item.title: Live markets: bitcoin leads crypto slide even as tech sector closes sharply higher
item.description: Oil prices and bond yields opened the week sharply lower following President Trump's weekend announcement of an imminent Middle East agreement.

item.title: Bitcoin Giant Strategy Slashes Cash Reserves by 61% to Repurchase $1.5 Billion in Debt
item.description: Strategy used 61% of its dedicated cash buffer to repurchase $1.5 billion in convertible notes, leaving its Bitcoin stash untouched.

item.title: Bitcoin’s big cup-and-handle pattern targets ‘minimum’ $220K BTC price
item.description: Bitcoin cup-and-handle chart breakout targets $220,000, but BTC price must first hold the $74,000 support area.

item.title: Crypto Industry Fights Senator Warren's Claim That Coinbase, Ripple Bank Charter Approvals Are Illegal
item.description: The Digital Chamber urged the OCC on Tuesday to defend its recent national bank charter approvals for crypto firms, which Sen. Elizabeth Warren slammed as improper.

item.title: Ethereum treasury firms lean on staking as ETF pressure builds: Report
item.description: Everstake said staking made up 60% of disclosed revenue among six Ethereum treasury firms, while loss-making companies posted $1.41 billion in losses.

item.title: Bitmine made its largest ETH purchase this year despite Tom Lee's slowdown suggestion
item.description: The company bought 111,942 ether last week worth $237 million, ramping up purchases to take advantage of ETH's drop below $2,200.

item.title: CoinDesk 20 performance update: SUI drops 1.1%, leading index lower
item.description: Cronos (CRO), also down 1.1%, joined Sui (SUI) as an underperformer.

item.title: Lucky Trader Rides $341 World Cup Meme Coin Buy to 14,000% Gains
item.description: The trader still has over $100,000 worth of unrealized gains in the World Cup token, ahead of the start of the tournament next month.

item.title: Strive acquires 1,109 bitcoin, raising total holdings to 16,500 coins
item.description: ASST climbs 133% in three months as Strive grows holdings to 16,500 BTC and explores fresh capital raising options.

item.title: Mysterious 107 BTC Sent to Bitcoin Burn Address in Five Transactions According to onchain monitor @SaniExp, someone sent 107 BTC worth roughly $8.2 million to Bitcoin’s well-known burn address 1111111111111111111114oLvT2 across five transaction - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiY0FVX3lxTE9BMjdGcWQ5eUgyY3V2NmdwbVNJWU9kVHRzamd5T3pmdlg1ck5rc19zMjNrTmNJbGN2eUJsbHVyWHFwRW0zTTVLRDVtN05XTHRkeTduN01XMDhsQmVraG5OTDFlVQ?oc=5" target="_blank">Mysterious 107 BTC Sent to Bitco…

item.title: Morning Minute: Trump Hints That Iran Deal May Be Close, Markets Rebound
item.description: Markets bounced back on Trump's “largely negotiated” U.S.-Iran deal, but further airstrikes underscore the fragile geopolitical position.

item.title: Bitcoin treasury firms add 603 BTC as Strategy pauses buying
item.description: Smaller Bitcoin treasury companies bought about $46 million worth of Bitcoin below $80,000 last week as the largest corporate holder paused its weekly buys.

item.title: Strategy taps cash reserve to retire $1.5 billion in convertible debt
item.description: Michael Saylor and team funded the repurchases using cash as it restructures liabilities tied to its BTC treasury strategy.

item.title: JUST IN: 🇺🇸 Public company Strive purchases 1,109 bitcoin, now holds 16,500 BTC worth $1.25 billion 🚀 - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiZ0FVX3lxTFB1T2JQX09sLVpVTGYtcEZYbHMybm5RNlV6aTN5SjM5MTB1SS0xbUJDS3JzdS0wTXZmWDR3VXpHcWVXRkhYUHJDOUt4MEt5eF9qZUozazh4aGkzQTN2ZGtGOTJzTGdjbnM?oc=5" target="_blank">JUST IN: 🇺🇸 Public compan…

item.title: Binance eyes Philippines return through SEC sandbox partnership
item.description: Binance remains blocked in the Philippines after a 2024 NTC directive tied to SEC concerns over licensing and registration.

item.title: Spain joins growing list of countries shutting out Polymarket and Kalshi
item.description: The platforms were blocked for operating without licenses required by Spanish gambling law, citing a lack of safeguards for minors and self-excluded gamblers.

item.title: Bitcoin demand gauge sinks to worst level since December as spot buying weakens
item.description: CryptoQuant’s 30-day apparent demand indicator is negative, signaling that buyers aren’t absorbing the available supply and leaving the market vulnerable.

item.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum ETFs Shed $112M as Hyperliquid Funds Extend 8-Day Win Streak
item.description: Hyperliquid ETFs posted an 8-day inflow streak as the network’s native token HYPE hit a new all-time high Sunday.

item.title: It’s the part of the cycle where any sort of experience in previous cycles gets derided as a handicap, but these blocks and their generational run is an example of how valuable it can be. We’ve been pointing out the inevitable impact to MLCCs since December - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiXEFVX3lxTE8zNTdoakw5RVU4Vm8xa3JhNVFjeTdqWGxKQmY5VnhzS3RyN3FtdnRubkMyR29fYjhrc0ZQSm9aVXBUam5raGkwSWVJdWNpeHVzNXZJbEZTdVV1T2Nt?oc=5" target="_blank">It’s the part of the cycle where any sort …

item.title: StablR freezes USDR and EURR after attacker mints $13.5 million in unbacked tokens
item.description: The breach, linked to a 1-of-3 multisig wallet weakness, allowed attackers to compromise a key and mint $13.5 million in unbacked tokens, netting them $2.8 million.

item.title: Bitcoin ETFs crushed by billions in outflows as Treasuries stifle interest-rate cut hopes
item.description: Your day-ahead look for May 26, 2026

item.title: Ex-Hodlnaut CEO charged with fraud over Terra exposure claims
item.description: Singapore police charge ex-Hodlnaut CEO Zhu Juntao with six counts of fraud by false representation over allegedly misleading statements on the company’s exposure to the Terra/UST crash.

item.title: Crypto funds bleed $1.47B as risk-off sentiment deepens
item.description: Crypto ETPs logged $1.47 billion of outflows last week as Bitcoin funds led losses, while nine altcoin ETPs still attracted inflows of more than $1 million.

item.title: Bitcoin risks another lower high as stocks rally, AI tokens outperform
item.description: The bitcoin price chart is forming a potential lower high while ether languishes in a months-old range even as S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 index futures push higher.
